title: Tender Jamaican Dough Stew
description: A floral and pillowy one-pot Jamaican dish featuring chicken stock and long-fermentation-dough, perfect for a flexitarian-friendly meal.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine flour, yeast, salt, sugar, and mix well.
Gradually add water to the dry ingredients and mix until a shaggy dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover it, and let it ferment in a warm place for 2 hours.
Heat oil in a large pot over medium heat, then add onion and garlic and saute until softened.
Add thyme, scotch bonnet pepper if using, and chicken stock to the pot, and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to low, add the fermented dough to the pot, and simmer for 20 minutes.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
Cook poultry to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F before serving.
